title: Connect and Operate MCP in Codex and Claude · Protocol Clinic: Discovery Smoke
summary: Configure one safe server in both Codex and Claude Code. Planned artifact: Add and verify the agent ext discovery smoke increment in cross-client MCP configuration and smoke tests. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Verify initialization, primitive discovery, tool calls, errors, and shutdown in each client.
Configure Claude Code local, project, or user MCP scope and review shared server trust.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

options.text: Reconstruct the conditions for Discovery Smoke, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.

steps.title: Build Discovery Smoke evidence
steps.instruction: Append a agent-ext-discovery-smoke-1n90fb: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Discovery Smoke into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
agent-ext-discovery-smoke-1n90fb:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

steps.title: Defend the Discovery Smoke model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Discovery Smoke applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Verify initialization, primitive discovery, tool calls, errors, and shutdown in each client.
